display_name: ICAR Indian Phytopathology — Wild brinjal (Solanum torvum) as a bacterial-wilt-resistant rootstock
publisher: Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) — Indian Phytopathology Journal
licence: ICAR journal — attribution for non-commercial use
notes: Peer-reviewed trial showing ~100% protection from Ralstonia wilt when susceptible brinjal is grafted onto wild Solanum torvum rootstock vs. 60–74% wilt in non-grafted seedlings. Strong candidate technique for Sri Lankan dry-zone tomato and brinjal, but rootstock sourcing and nursery grafting capacity are the limiting factors.
